back_translation: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth, because the original heaven and the original earth had passed away, and the sea no longer existed
And I saw the holy city, namely the new Jerusalem, descending from heaven, from God, prepared like a bride who has adorned herself for her bridegroom

And I heard a loud voice from the throne, saying, ‘Behold, the dwelling-place of God is together with humanity, and He will dwell with them, and they will be the people of His, and God Himself will be with them
And He will wipe every drop of tear from their eyes, and death will be no more, and grief, weeping, and pain will not exist anymore. The original things have passed away’
And the One who was seated on the throne said, ‘Behold, I make all things new.’ And He said, ‘Write, because these words are faithful and true’
And He said to me, ‘It is finished. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last. I will give to the one who is thirsty to drink from the spring of water that gives life, without cost
The one who conquers will receive these things as inheritance, and I will be God to him, and he will be a son to Me

But for the cowardly, the unbelieving, the abominable, murderers, sexually-immoral, sorcerers, idolaters, and all who tell lies, their portion will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death’
And one of the seven angels, who held the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues, came and said to me, ‘Come, I will show you the bride, namely the wife of the Lamb’
And the angel carried me away by the Spirit, up to a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city, namely Jerusalem, descending from heaven, from God
That city had the glory of God. The light of the city was like the most precious jewel, like jasper-jewel clear as crystal
That city had a great and high wall, with twelve city-gates, and at those gates there were twelve angels, and there were names inscribed, namely the names of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel
On the east side there were three gates, on the north side three gates, on the south side three gates, and on the west side three gates
And the wall of that city had twelve foundation-tiers, and on those foundations were the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb
And the one who was speaking with me had a golden measuring-rod, in order to measure that city, the gates of the city, and the wall of the city
And that city was a square shape, the length equal to the width. And the angel measured the city with the measuring-rod, getting twelve-thousand stadia. The length, width, and height were equal
And the angel measured the wall of the city, getting one-hundred-forty-four cubits, according to human measure, which is also the angel's measure
And the building-material of the wall of the city was jasper-jewel, and that city was pure gold, looking transparent like clear glass

The foundations of the wall of the city were adorned with every kind of precious jewel. The first foundation was jasper-jewel, the second sapphire-jewel, the third chalcedony-jewel, the fourth emerald

The fifth was sardonyx-jewel, the sixth ruby-jewel, the seventh chrysolite-jewel, the eighth beryl-jewel, the ninth topaz-jewel, the tenth chrysoprase-jewel, the eleventh jacinth-jewel, the twelfth amethyst-jewel
And the twelve gates were twelve pearls, each gate was a single pearl. And the streets of the city were pure gold, looking transparent like glass
And I did not see a temple in the city, because the Lord God, the One who possesses supreme authority, and the Lamb, are the temple of the city
And the city does not need the sun or moon to shine in the city, because the glory of God gives light to the city, and the Lamb is the lamp of the city
And the nations will walk by the light of the city, and the kings of the earth will bring their splendor into the city
And the gates of the city will not be shut during the daytime, because there will be no nighttime there
And they will bring the splendor and honor of the nations into the city
And nothing that is defiled, or those who do detestable things or speak falsehood, will enter the city. Except those whose names are inscribed in the book of life of the Lamb
